key_insights.insight: Looking for better ways to do things, rather than just accepting the status quo, is a key trait for entrepreneurs.
key_insights.quote: I look at the way something's done and immediately start thinking of better ways to do it rather than just doing what I'm told.

key_insights.insight: Naivety can be a strength for founders, preventing them from being discouraged by the high failure rates and past attempts.
key_insights.quote: Actually the benefit of being naive and even being quite young in your career is you don't have that baggage of knowing how it failed the five times before, which I find super interesting.

key_insights.insight: Technical ability, especially coding, provides a significant advantage to founders in the early stages of a company.
key_insights.quote: Being technical being able to write code i think is just a huge huge leg up and all of the founders who aren't technical... i think their biggest problem at the early stage is finding a technical co-founder.

key_insights.insight: Determination and resilience are critical for overcoming obstacles and achieving success in entrepreneurship.
key_insights.quote: Being really, really determined and resilient... being indefatigable basically i think is is the singlebiggest predictor of success.

key_insights.insight: Confidence can decrease with experience as one becomes more aware of the potential for failure.
key_insights.quote: I was more confident when I was 28 than I am now for sure and I think that comes with experience um I think you you take enough knocks that you start to and you realize you don't know.

key_insights.insight: Privilege plays a role in the ability to take risks, as a safety net allows for more experimentation.
key_insights.quote: I knew I could take risk because if that risk didn't pay off, I have that safety net.

actionable_advice.how_to: Take online courses, attend coding bootcamps, or pursue a computer science degree. Focus on practical application by building websites and projects.
Evaluate potential downsides and ensure you have a safety net to fall back on before taking big life or career steps. Start with smaller risks to build confidence.

memorable_stories: Story of automating a jewelry website tally task with an Excel script, highlighting inefficiency in billing practices.
Story of quitting a consulting job three days before starting to pursue a Y Combinator startup offer.
contrarian_claims: That naivety and a lack of experience can be advantageous in entrepreneurship.
People in privileged countries don't take enough risk.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 30 - These Qualities Will Help You In Business: Tom Blomfield
